hypothesis.statement: A probabilistic decision-support layer can improve the quality, efficiency, and learning of repeated decisions in STOCK_ZERO and the multi-agent flow, above static rules or deterministic scorecards, without measurement/maintenance/complexity cost exceeding the benefit.

executive_conclusion: There is real, repeated uncertainty in STOCK_ZERO, so a Bayesian layer is not rejected outright. But no decision surface currently satisfies all justification criteria at once: the strongest technical seam (data-load batch anomaly scoring) is limited by small sample and sparse failure labels; the highest-value product seams (alert->action efficacy, operational prioritization) have no live feedback loop because action/outcome tracking is not implemented; AI-workflow and engineering decisions are largely non-exchangeable or already rules-sufficient; and every product/CG decision surface inspected (focos, CUMPLE/INCUMPLE) is deterministic by design today. The proportionate move is DO_NOT_BUILD now, with at most a cheap, private, append-only observation contract on the one strong seam so the question becomes answerable later. Conceptual priority is modest; instrumentation priority is the only one that could start; implementation and production priorities are not warranted now.

where_uncertainty_is_material_and_repeat: Data-load reliability: per-batch anomaly judgement (row-count/new-key/duplicate/drift) repeats every load and is materially risky to parity.
Operational efficacy: whether an action on an alert/foco actually resolves it next period (repeats often, high value).
where_decisions_are_actually_determinist: Inventory focos: Venta(+7)==0, NEGATIVO=='SI', RIESGO DE QUIEBRE=='SI' are boolean flags (app/screens/reposicion.py _row_indicadores).
Control Gestion alerts: CUMPLE/INCUMPLE derived from capped valid visits vs plan (app/screens/control_gestion.py).
Safe-load go/no-go: staged-validate-then-commit is an exact gate (refresh_control_gestion_v2_incremental.py).
Canonical build parity: deterministic hashes, 1 variant across 3 runs + clean-room (C007).
where_there_is_no_feedback_loop_yet: decision_and_action_tracking is a FUTURE_CAPABILITY with empty current_implementation; no action/outcome ledger exists, so alert->action efficacy cannot be learned today.
problem_validation.evidence_quality: Based on validated research files (C001/C002/C006/C007), the capability map, and static reading of app screens; no DB access and no new measurement were performed.

alternatives_compared.name: RULES_ONLY
alternatives_compared.description: Static rules, gates, and human decisions.
alternatives_compared.fit_today: STRONG for safety/irreversible and one-off decisions (gates, go/no-go, schema contracts).
alternatives_compared.limitation: Does not adapt thresholds to per-source variability or learn from outcomes.

alternatives_compared.name: DETERMINISTIC_SCORECARD
alternatives_compared.description: Defined scores and fixed thresholds, no probabilistic updating.
alternatives_compared.fit_today: GOOD as the next step for batch-anomaly bands and build/reuse/retire checklists.
alternatives_compared.limitation: Fixed thresholds ignore uncertainty and do not calibrate; can over/under-alert under varying batch size.

alternatives_compared.name: BAYESIAN_DECISION_SUPPORT
alternatives_compared.description: Belief updating, uncertainty quantification, expected-utility decisions.
alternatives_compared.fit_today: NOT_YET: best theoretical fit for UC-DL-01 and UC-PA-02 but blocked by sample size / missing feedback loop.
alternatives_compared.limitation: Wide posteriors with current n; measurement and maintenance cost not yet repaid.

alternatives_compared.name: HYBRID
alternatives_compared.description: Hard rules for safety; Bayes only for the genuinely uncertain sub-decision.
alternatives_compared.fit_today: PREFERRED EVENTUAL SHAPE for UC-DL-01 (rules keep loads safe; a calibrated band flags only the uncertain middle).
alternatives_compared.limitation: Still requires the observation contract and labels before any model is fit.

alternatives_compared.name: DO_NOT_BUILD
alternatives_compared.description: Keep current governance because incremental benefit does not justify complexity.
alternatives_compared.fit_today: CORRECT FOR NOW across all domains; pairs with a low-cost observation contract on the strongest seam.
alternatives_compared.limitation: Risks indefinite deferral; mitigated by a concrete re-evaluation trigger.

domain_assessments.finding: High decision frequency but non-comparable units, continuous process change, coarse/confounded outcome labels, and high measurement cost. Safety-critical validation is already a correct hard rule.

Phase and architecture decisions are largely one-off and path-dependent (not exchangeable). Build/reuse/retire is at most a deterministic scorecard. Value-of-information is better handled by an explicit gate-criteria stop rule.

The single strongest seam (UC-DL-01 batch anomaly) is exchangeable, observable, and its metrics are already logged in batch_registry (37 CG batches, 19 route batches) - but failure labels are sparse and per-source n is small, so a distribution would not yet beat a fixed band. Schema-change risk and safe-load go/no-go are rules-sufficient.
domain_assessments.recommended_alternative: HYBRID (eventual) preceded by OBSERVE_ONLY; rules sufficient elsewhere

domain_assessments.finding: Today's product/CG surfaces are deterministic flags. The genuinely probabilistic questions (action efficacy, dead-SKU, prioritization resolution) all require an action/outcome ledger that is not implemented (decision_and_action_tracking = FUTURE_CAPABILITY) and, for worker-level efficacy, privacy gating and a causal design.
Evaluated as a conceptual contract only. Highest privacy risk. Must remain in private, git-ignored storage, fully separate from the technical rail. No real personal data recorded in this assessment.

conceptual.rationale: Worth keeping the probabilistic lens alive as a way of thinking about load anomalies and action efficacy; it should not yet compete with operational lanes (RUTA_RUTERO, KPIONE2, frequency, CG-005).

instrumentation.rationale: The only tier that could legitimately start: a cheap, private, append-only observation log on the strongest seam (UC-DL-01) so a future evaluation has comparable data. Must not touch operational rails, DB writes, loaders, or Supabase.

implementation.rationale: No model should be built until the observation contract yields enough comparable labels and a distribution is shown to beat a fixed rule.

production.rationale: No production probabilistic surface is warranted; it would also collide with active gates (G0 parity, CG-005 shadow) and forbidden actions.

first_finding_answer.question: Should the Bayesian layer become a parallel project priority now, while RUTA_RUTERO and frequency loads continue?
first_finding_answer.answer: NO as a parallel build priority. The benefit of starting now is low (no live feedback loop, small samples), the distraction and over-engineering cost is real, and it depends on data that does not yet exist. The only defensible parallel activity is a low-cost observation contract that does not compete with the operational lanes.
first_finding_answer.value_of_waiting: HIGH while batch labels and any action ledger are absent; re-evaluate when the observation triggers are met.

no_pilot_reason: NO_PILOT_YET. No use case currently satisfies all justification criteria simultaneously: UC-DL-01 (strongest) is sample- and label-limited; UC-PA-01/02/03 lack any live feedback loop because action/outcome tracking is unimplemented. Selecting a model-fitting pilot now would violate proportionality. The recommended next step is an observation/measurement contract (instrumentation), not a Bayesian pilot, with an explicit trigger to revisit. The first pilot, WHEN justified, would be a shadow, read-only, retrospective HYBRID anomaly band for UC-DL-01.

candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.concrete_problem: Flag a freshly loaded source batch as anomalous (vs accept) using per-source predictive bands on row-count, new-key rate, and duplicate rate.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.minimum_observation: Per-source batch metrics already in batch_registry plus a small labeled good/bad set.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.outcome: Later-confirmed parity break / drift / duplicate explosion for the batch.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.utility: Catch a bad batch before it pollutes parity, with few false positives.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.measurement_cost: LOW (data already logged); labeling cost is the real cost.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.proposed_update: Beta-Binomial / Poisson predictive band per source; hard rules retained for safety (HYBRID).
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.success_criterion: On retrospective replay, the band flags all labeled bad batches AND beats a fixed percentage band on false-positive rate at equal recall.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.failure_criterion: Posteriors too wide to separate good/bad, or no improvement over a fixed band.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.abandon_criterion: After the observation window, fewer than the minimum labeled anomalies per source accrue, or rules already catch every labeled case.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.minimum_observations: ~20-30 clean batches per source AND >=3-5 labeled anomalies per source.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.estimated_horizon: Defer until enough batches/labels accrue from normal operations; do not accelerate loads to gather data.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.risk: Over-engineering; alert fatigue; look-ahead leakage if labels are derived from post-hoc parity.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.later_implementation_required: Shadow, read-only retrospective only; no production wiring without separate authorization.
candidate_first_pilot_when_justified.reversibility: HIGH (pure analysis over historical evidence; nothing applied).

measurement_contract.purpose: Make the strongest seam answerable later without building a model now.
what_to_record: per batch: source, batch_id, loaded_rows, selected_window_rows, new_key_rate, duplicate_rate, snapshot_hash, declared window/route/precedence/build versions
post-hoc label: clean | anomalous (with reason), assigned by human/Codex review, recorded WITHOUT look-ahead into the same parity used as the feature
measurement_contract.storage: Append-only research artifact (e.g., a future research/ ledger), local only, no DB writes, no Supabase, no loader execution.
explicitly_excluded: No new loads or refreshes triggered to gather data
No production writes, no payload_json changes, no cleanup/retention
No coupling to RUTA_RUTERO / KPIONE2 / frequency operational lanes
measurement_contract.reevaluation_trigger: When >=20-30 clean + >=3-5 labeled anomalous batches per source exist, run a retrospective shadow comparison (fixed band vs predictive band) and re-decide.

privacy_boundary.technical_rail: All use cases except UC-PG-01; batch/parity metadata only, no PII.
privacy_boundary.personal_growth_rail: UC-PG-01 is conceptual only; must live in private, git-ignored storage, separate from technical memory.
rules: No real personal data written to Git or shared memory.
Worker-level efficacy data (UC-PA-02) is sensitive and requires explicit privacy gating before any capture.
No secrets, DSNs, or credentials in any artifact.

causal_limits.note: UC-PA-03 dead-SKU is prediction; useful only if a decision consumes the probability.
UC-DL-01 anomaly scoring is decision support over associations, not causal.
Batch metric drift correlates with problems; correlation is sufficient for flagging, not for cause.
UC-PA-02 action efficacy is a causal question; observational compliance deltas are confounded by route/person changes and regression to the mean.
Any 'would compliance have improved without the action' claim needs a design (panel/hierarchical, synthetic control), which is explicitly NOT designed here.

risks: Distraction from active operational lanes (RUTA_RUTERO load, KPIONE2 format, frequency loads, CG-005 shadow).
Over-engineering: building probability where deterministic rules already suffice.
Small-sample over-confidence: wide posteriors presented as precision.
Feedback-loop illusion: scoring decisions whose outcomes are never recorded.
Privacy leakage if the personal-growth rail merges with the technical rail or enters Git.
Causal overreach: treating observational efficacy as proven effect.
falsification_conditions: NOT_YET_JUSTIFIED would flip toward SUPPORTED_FOR_PILOT if a seam shows, on real logged data, that a calibrated distribution changes a repeated decision AND beats a fixed rule at equal recall.
Specifically for UC-DL-01: accumulate >=20-30 clean + >=3-5 labeled anomalous batches per source and show a predictive band strictly dominating a fixed band on the FP/recall frontier.
For UC-PA-02: once an action/outcome ledger exists with a valid design, show a credibly estimated effect that changes prioritization.
The verdict would move toward REJECTED if, after instrumentation, deterministic rules match the probabilistic layer on every labeled case (probability changes no decision).
The verdict is wrong if any inspected 'deterministic' surface (focos, CUMPLE/INCUMPLE) actually already consumes a hidden probability - re-check the SQL/runtime would falsify the determinism claim.

next_decision.owner: Bastian (final authority); Codex independent validation first
next_decision.decision: Do NOT start a Bayesian build now. Optionally authorize ONLY a low-cost, private, append-only observation contract on UC-DL-01, kept off the operational lanes.
next_decision.if_approved_scope: Design-only / read-only retrospective; no DB, no loaders, no Supabase, no production apply.
next_decision.reevaluate_when: Observation triggers for UC-DL-01 are met, or an action/outcome ledger is independently built for product seams.

quality_assessment.contract_adherence: All five alternatives compared; five domains assessed; falsification conditions provided; pilot allowed to be null; implementation_authorized=false; no files modified outside the three authorized artifacts; no DB/loader/SQL executed; no personal data written.
quality_assessment.confidence_basis: Verdict rests on stable, validated facts (deterministic focos/alerts, empty action-tracking capability, logged-but-small batch history, deterministic C007 parity). Main uncertainty is residual: no DB access and no fresh measurement, so sample-size and label-sparsity claims come from the evidence files, not new counts.
unresolved_items: Exact per-source count of historically labeled bad batches (needs read-only batch_registry inspection in an authorized phase).
Whether any downstream consumer already wants a probability (re-check app/db.py SQL in an authorized phase).
Codex independent validation of this assessment.

evidence_classification.claim: Inventory focos and CG alerts are deterministic flags today.
evidence_classification.falsification_condition: Find probability/threshold-with-uncertainty logic feeding those flags.

evidence_classification.claim: No action/outcome ledger exists; decision_and_action_tracking is FUTURE_CAPABILITY with empty implementation.
Batch metrics are already logged but the population is small and failure labels are sparse.
evidence_classification.evidence_source: AI_SHARED_MEMORY c001 LOADER-FACT-002 (37 batches) and RUTA-FACT-002 (19 batches); C007 snapshot drift example
evidence_classification.falsification_condition: Read-only batch_registry shows ample labeled anomalies per source.

evidence_classification.claim: Data-load anomaly is the strongest Bayes seam.
evidence_classification.evidence_source: Use-case matrix scoring; exchangeability + logged metrics + observability
evidence_classification.falsification_condition: Another seam shows a live feedback loop with sufficient comparable data.

evidence_classification.claim: A probabilistic layer is not justified as a parallel build priority now.
evidence_classification.falsification_condition: A seam demonstrably beats rules on logged data.
